title: Cape Verde FIFA World Cup 2026 preview — Can the Blue Sharks spring a surprise?
original_text: Cape Verde FIFA World Cup 2026 preview — Can the Blue Sharks spring a surprise? left Dailon Livramento is expected to lead the line for Cape Verde. AP https://ss-i.thgim.com/public/incoming/vpwzkq/article71080739.ece/alternates/FREE_1200/WCup_Group_H_Preview_Soccer_25_12.jpg SHANE WENZLICK FILE - Cape Verde's Dailon Livramento celebrates after scoring a goal against Chile during their international soccer friendly in Auckland, New Zealand, Friday, March 27, 2026. (Shane Wenzlick/photosport via AP, File) Cape Verde enters the tournament as an underdog, but has secured creditable victories over Cameroon and Ghana in the last two years. Chennai The 2010 world champion will face debutant Cape Verde in its opener, which could see Spain go with a rotated squad. The tiny African team, despite entering the tournament as an underdog, has beaten Cameroon and Ghana in the last two years. Under the tutelage of former player-turned-manager Pedro Leitão Brito, popularly known as Bubista, it aims to build on its reputation as a giant killer on the global stage. The Blue Sharks will also expect miracles from captain Ryan Mendes, their record goalscorer, while Sidny Lopes Cabral, who honed his defensive skills under Jose Mourinho at Benfica, and Logan Costa, who plays for Villarreal, will man the defence. Jovane Cabral, who spent a decade at Sporting CP, winning six trophies, will shoulder the responsibility of progressive carries along the flanks. Playing against a high-intensity Spanish side in its World Cup opener, Cape Verde might choose to park the bus, but with 16 goals in 10 games in its qualification campaign, it has proved it has enough arms in its arsenal to hurt the opponent on the counter. Player to watch out for: Dailon Livramento The 25-year-old forward was Cape Verde’s leading goalscorer in the World Cup qualifiers — four goals — and plays in the Portuguese top-flight, bringing elite European experience to the table. A much younger and faster alternative to captain Ryan Mendes, he is also known to use his aerial ability — he has a frame of 185cm — for set-piece situations. Cape Verde World Cup Stats: FIFA ranking: 67 Appearances: 0 Best finish: Yet to play Matches: 0 Wins: 0 Draws: 0 Losses: 0 Goals for: 0 Goals against: 0 Highest World Cup goalscorer in squad: Ryan Mendes (22 goals) Coach: Bubista, who took charge of Cape Verde in 2020, has transformed the side from being a mere minnow to a promising side in Africa. In 2023, it beat heavyweight Ghana and drew with Egypt to reach the quarterfinals of the African Cup of Nations 2023, only to be knocked out on penalties by South Africa. center Bubista took charge of Cape Verde in 2020. AP Photo/Sunday Alamba https://ss-i.thgim.com/public/incoming/l9oegk/article71080744.ece/alternates/FREE_1200/WCup_Group_H_Preview_Soccer__8_32.jpg Sunday Alamba FILE - Cape Verde's head coach Bubista is pictured before the African Cup of Nations Group B soccer match between Ghana and Cape Verde in Abidjan, Ivory Coast, Sunday, Jan. 14, 2024. (AP Photo/Sunday Alamba, File) Playing with an attacking 4-3-3 shape, Bubista’s men lost just once in the World Cup qualification, while beating Cameroon 1-0 at home. Entering the World Cup for the first time, either as a player or coach, the 56-year-old will look to execute his plan to perfection. Cape Verde Squad: Goalkeepers: Carlos dos Santos, Marcio Rosa, Vozinha Defenders: Sidny Cabral, Diney Borges, Logan Costa, Roberto Lopes, Steven Moreira, Wagner Pina, Kelvin Pires, Stopira Midfielders: Telmo Arcanjo, Deroy Duarte, Laros Duarte, João Paulo Fernandes, Jamiro Monteiro, Kevin Pina, Yannick Semedo Forwards: Gilson Benchimol, Jovane Cabral, Dailon Livramento, Ryan Mendes, Nuno da Costa, Garry Rodrigues, Willy Semedo, Helio Varela Cape Verde Predicted XI (4-3-3): Vozinha — Sidny Cabral, Logan Costa, Roberto Lopes, Steven Moreira — Telmo Arcanjo, Yannick Semedo, Deroy Duarte — Jovane Cabral, Dailon Livramento, Ryan Mende Cape Verde World Cup Stats: FIFA ranking: 67 Appearances: 0 Best finish: Yet to play Matches: 0 Wins: 0 Draws: 0 Losses: 0 Goals for: 0 Goals against: 0 Highest World Cup goalscorer in squad: Ryan Mendes (22 goals) Cape Verde Squad: Goalkeepers: Carlos dos Santos, Marcio Rosa, Vozinha Defenders: Sidny Cabral, Diney Borges, Logan Costa, Roberto Lopes, Steven Moreira, Wagner Pina, Kelvin Pires, Stopira Midfielders: Telmo Arcanjo, Deroy Duarte, Laros Duarte, João Paulo Fernandes, Jamiro Monteiro, Kevin Pina, Yannick Semedo Forwards: Gilson Benchimol, Jovane Cabral, Dailon Livramento, Ryan Mendes, Nuno da Costa, Garry Rodrigues, Willy Semedo, Helio Varela Cape Verde Predicted XI (4-3-3): Vozinha — Sidny Cabral, Logan Costa, Roberto Lopes, Steven Moreira — Telmo Arcanjo, Yannick Semedo, Deroy Duarte — Jovane Cabral, Dailon Livramento, Ryan Mende
